Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Sarah, thanks for working on this issue.  It looks like there was one conf-sanity test_32g failure on the Gerrit Janitor testing:

 Check FLR file
 dd: failed to open '/tmp/t32/mnt/lustre/tmp_file': No space left on device

but I'm not sure if this is a problem in the test image running out of space, or the test node itself?

I will rebase the patch to rerun these tests, so that we can see if this is a one-time failure, or if it is failing regularly.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

Do we need to skip these new tests if we are running with ZFS?

Do we need to skip these tests if we have a separate MDS and MGS? I know that several of the 32* tests are skipped in this configuration.

Patch Set 2:

> Do we need to skip these new tests if we are running with ZFS?
 > 
 > Do we need to skip these tests if we have a separate MDS and MGS? I
 > know that several of the 32* tests are skipped in this
 > configuration.

The t32_check() generates a list of the matching disk*$FSTYPE* images, so no need to explicitly skip them for the non-matching FSTYPE.

I couldn't say for sure about separate MGT/MDT, but I _suspect_ that the test configuration needs to match the test image, so the skips should probably be the same.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as f2143c0790bb1cb802fad7e81bcb386dc0245b36 by Oleg Drokin

commit.subject: LU-11643 tests: add new images and tests for upgrade tests
commit.message:
LU-11643 tests: add new images and tests for upgrade tests

Add new images for conf-sanity.sh 32

disk2_10-ldiskfs.tar.bz2
disk2_12-ldiskfs.tar.bz2

Test-Parameters: trivial
Test-Parameters: fstype=ldiskfs envdefinitions=ONLY="32f 32g" testlist=conf-sanity

Change-Id: I6682e247308d7cf3fb57eee595751d6d140a421f
Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <sarah@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/45827
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
